calendar.matters:
Hearing On Plaintiffs' Motion To Apportion Settlement Funds.
Off Calendar Jul-15-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on plaintiff's Motion to Apportion Settlement Funds.
Dept. 218, Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Catherine Fuller, et al.
Charles Neal, Lon Fuller and Lee Fuller were present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court Orders that the following are entitled to distribution of all net settlement funds from the underlying survival and wrongful death actions in the following proportions:

Estate of Donald Fuller - Asbestos Injured Party - 0%
Estate of Catherine Fuller - Surviving Party - 0%
Estate of Lillian Nelson - Daughter, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Leland Fuller - Son, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Leroy Fuller - Son, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Estate of Linda Neal - Daughter, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Lon Fuller, Son, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Lee Fuller, Son, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Lorrie Dalbec, Daughter, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%
Lyle Fuller, Son, as Wrongful Death Heir - 12.5%

Brayton Purcell LLP shall recover from the net settlement funds of $17,423.06, currently in Prayton Purcell LLP's possession, the sum of $1,899.00 as reasonable attorney fees & costs, leaving the net amount of $15,524.06. Any future net settlement funds shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of Charles Neal shall be sufficient on all settlement documents with any defendants in the underlying asbestos survival and wrongful death action.

Order Granting Motion to Distribute Settlement Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.
